blocks.text: Agricultural Sciences is the study of farming systems, plants, animals, soil, water, and the way people produce food and raw materials.
Agriculture is important because it supports food production, jobs, trade, and rural development. It also connects science with daily life.

blocks.alt: Farm scene with crops and animals
blocks.caption: Agriculture includes crops, animals, soil, water, and technology.

blocks.text: A modern farmer needs knowledge, planning, and responsible use of natural resources.

agriculture.definition: The practice and science of farming, including crops and animals.

resources.definition: Useful materials or supplies such as soil, water, money, or tools.

worksheet.title: Introducing Agriculture
worksheet.prompt:
1. What is agriculture?

2. Why is agriculture important in society?

3. Name two things a farmer must manage well.

blocks.text: Soil is one of the most important natural resources in agriculture. Plants grow in soil, and soil provides water, air, and nutrients.
Healthy [[soil]] supports strong plant growth. Poor soil can reduce crop production.

blocks.caption: Different layers of soil make up a soil profile.

blocks.text: Farmers need to understand soil texture, soil structure, drainage, and fertility.

soil.definition: The upper layer of the earth in which plants grow.

fertility.definition: The ability of soil to support plant growth by providing nutrients.

worksheet.title: Why Soil Matters
worksheet.prompt:
1. Why is soil important in agriculture?

2. What does healthy soil provide for plants?

3. What is soil fertility?

ch3.title: Chapter 3: Soil Formation and Soil Types

blocks.text: Soil forms over a long time as rocks break down and organic matter mixes with minerals. Climate, living organisms, slope, parent material, and time all affect soil formation.
Different soils have different properties. Sandy soil drains quickly, clay soil holds more water, and loam is often suitable for agriculture because it balances drainage and water retention.
Understanding soil types helps farmers choose the right crop and management method.

parent material.definition: The original rock or material from which soil develops.

loam.definition: A balanced soil type made of sand, silt, and clay.

worksheet.title: Soil Types
worksheet.prompt:
1. Name two factors that affect soil formation.

2. How is sandy soil different from clay soil?

3. Why is loam useful in agriculture?

blocks.text: Plants need water for growth, transport of nutrients, and photosynthesis. Without enough water, plants become weak and may not produce well.
Farmers must use water wisely. Too little water causes stress, while too much water can damage roots and reduce oxygen in the soil.

blocks.caption: Irrigation helps supply water to crops when rainfall is not enough.

photosynthesis.definition: The process by which green plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to make food.

irrigation.definition: The supply of water to crops by artificial means.

worksheet.title: Water on the Farm
worksheet.prompt:
1. Why do plants need water?

2. What can happen if plants get too little water?

3. What is irrigation?

blocks.text: Plants need nutrients to grow well. Important nutrients include nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium.
Nitrogen supports leaf growth, phosphorus helps roots and flowers, and potassium helps general plant health.
If a plant lacks nutrients, it may show signs such as yellow leaves, weak stems, or poor growth.

nutrients.definition: Substances needed for growth and health.

nitrogen.definition: A nutrient that helps plants grow leaves and stems.

worksheet.title: Feeding Plants
worksheet.prompt:
1. What are nutrients?

2. Name three important plant nutrients.

3. What signs may show that a plant lacks nutrients?

ch6.title: Chapter 6: Farm Animals and Their Importance

blocks.text: Farm animals are important in agriculture because they provide food, income, manure, fibre, and labour in some farming systems.
Common farm animals include cattle, sheep, goats, pigs, and poultry.

blocks.caption: Farm animals support food production and livelihoods.

blocks.text: Farmers must provide animals with food, clean water, shelter, and health care.

poultry.definition: Domestic birds kept for eggs or meat, such as chickens.

livelihoods.definition: Ways in which people earn a living.

worksheet.title: Animals in Agriculture
worksheet.prompt:
1. Why are farm animals important?

2. Name four common farm animals.

3. What do animals need to stay healthy?

blocks.text: Animals need balanced feed to grow, stay healthy, and produce well. Feed provides energy, protein, vitamins, minerals, and water.
Different animals need different types of feed depending on their age, size, and purpose.
Poor nutrition can lead to slow growth, illness, and low production.

protein.definition: A nutrient important for growth and repair in living organisms.

minerals.definition: Important substances needed in small amounts for healthy growth and body function.

worksheet.title: Feeding Animals
worksheet.prompt:
1. Why is balanced feed important?

2. Name three things feed provides.

3. What can happen if an animal has poor nutrition?

blocks.text: Sustainable farming means producing food in a way that protects natural resources and supports future production.
This includes conserving soil, using water wisely, avoiding waste, and managing animals and crops responsibly.
Sustainability is important because agriculture depends on the environment.

sustainable.definition: Able to continue over time without damaging important resources.

conserving.definition: Protecting and using resources carefully so they are not wasted.

worksheet.title: Farming for the Future
worksheet.prompt:
1. What is sustainable farming?

2. Why is sustainability important in agriculture?

3. Name two ways a farmer can protect resources.

blocks.text: Agriculture offers many career paths. People can work as farmers, animal scientists, soil scientists, technicians, agricultural advisors, and researchers.
Modern agriculture also uses technology, business planning, and scientific research.
This means agriculture is not only about working in fields. It is also about solving problems, managing systems, and improving food production.

researchers.definition: People who study and investigate topics carefully to discover new knowledge.

technicians.definition: People skilled in practical and technical work.

worksheet.title: Careers in Agriculture
worksheet.prompt:
1. Name three careers in agriculture.

2. Why is agriculture connected to science and technology?

3. Which agricultural career sounds interesting to you, and why?

ch10.title: Chapter 10: Looking Back at Agricultural Sciences

blocks.text: In this book you learned about agriculture, soil, water, nutrients, animals, sustainability, and careers.
Agricultural Sciences helps learners understand how food is produced and why natural resources must be managed carefully.
The subject also teaches responsibility, observation, scientific thinking, and respect for life and the environment.

environment.definition: The natural world around us, including land, water, air, plants, and animals.

worksheet.title: Review and Reflect
worksheet.prompt:
1. Name two things you learned from this book.

2. Why is soil important in agriculture?

3. Why should farmers protect natural resources?
